assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an accessible email inbox.
No ServiceNow ID, Developer Site account, or Personal Developer Instance exists yet.
The developer has not preselected App Engine Studio, UI Builder, Flow Designer, or an external API client as the first surface.

developer_goal: Create a ServiceNow Developer Program identity and reach the earliest meaningful first-success outcome on a chosen Now Platform development surface.

first_success.outcome: For the REST API Explorer candidate on a Personal Developer Instance: create a test incident through Table API v1 and retrieve that incident by sys_id.
first_success.observable_signal: The POST response includes status and a Location header with sys_id; the following GET response body contains the inserted incident record.

route_selection.surface: ServiceNow Developer Site with free Personal Developer Instance
route_selection.rule: Prefer the free Developer Program PDI path over production-customer entitlements when both can reach the selected developer outcome. Do not invent a winner among REST Explorer, App Engine, and other surfaces.

candidates.name: Developer Site signup, Admin-role PDI, REST API Explorer Table API create-and-read
candidates.reason: Official for REST Explorer, but PDI release inventory and the Admin versus App Engine Studio Creator role choice change reachability.

candidates.name: App Engine Studio Creator role and App Engine first app
candidates.reason: First-sign-in role choice can restrict the PDI away from unrestricted admin and REST Explorer access.

candidates.name: Customer or partner production/non-production instance instead of PDI
candidates.reason: Requires organization entitlement outside the free Developer Program path.

required_path.action: Create a ServiceNow ID from the Developer Site Get a ServiceNow ID signup form.
required_path.observable_result: ServiceNow accepts signup and sends the Developer Program welcome email.

required_path.action: Open the Welcome to ServiceNow from Developer Program email and select Verify Email.
required_path.observable_result: The email address is verified and login becomes available.

required_path.action: Complete first-sign-in role setup, including job-responsibility choice and Developer site terms acceptance.
required_path.observable_result: The Developer Site account is ready and a user role is assigned for PDI access.

required_path.action: Request a Personal Developer Instance for an offered ServiceNow release and open it with Start Building.
required_path.observable_result: A PDI URL and credentials are assigned and the instance UI is reachable.

required_path.action: Open REST API Explorer, create an incident with Table API v1, then GET the incident by sys_id.
required_path.observable_result: POST and GET responses show the inserted incident record.

prerequisites.description: The rest_api_explorer role or admin role is required to use REST API Explorer.
PDI access depends on keeping the Developer Site account active; inactivity can reclaim the instance.

external_gates.description: Email verification is required before first Developer Site login.
First-sign-in Admin versus App Engine Studio Creator role choice gates unrestricted PDI capabilities.
PDI assignment depends on currently offered releases and available inventory.

unavoidable_waits.description: ServiceNow must deliver the verification email before login.
PDI provisioning may wait until an instance is assigned after request.

platform_outcomes.description: ServiceNow creates the Developer Site account after verified signup.
A requested PDI receives a URL and admin credentials when inventory is available.
REST API Explorer executes Table API create and read against the instance.

excluded.item: Reading Now Platform conceptual documentation beyond the Explorer tutorial
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action.

excluded.item: External REST clients outside REST API Explorer
excluded.reason: Alternate interface to the same Table API outcome.
Official Explorer guidance warns against production use and the free path uses a PDI.

sources.title: Access the REST API Explorer
evidence_supported: How to open Explorer and view API resources

sources.title: Create an incident with REST API Explorer

uncertainties.question: Which ServiceNow development surface should represent the organization-wide first mile: REST API Explorer, App Engine Studio, UI Builder, Flow Designer, or another product API?
uncertainties.impact: Each surface has a different first success and may require a different PDI role.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded developer goal naming one surface, plus confirmation that the Admin-role PDI path is acceptable for that goal.

uncertainties.question: Which PDI releases and capacity will be available at request time, and how long does assignment take?
uncertainties.impact: Unavailable inventory can block or delay the path before any Explorer work begins.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated PDI request attempt recording offered releases, wait time, and whether Start Building immediately yields Admin access.

uncertainties.question: Does selecting the App Engine Studio Creator path at first sign-in prevent the REST Explorer create-and-read terminal without an extra role change?
uncertainties.impact: An early role choice can force a detour or a different first success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official guidance or a walkthrough showing Admin versus Creator consequences for REST API Explorer.
